criteria: The agent identifies that the core claim is a linear representation of U.S. political ideology in LLM activation space across Llama-2-7b-chat, Mistral-7b-instruct, and Vicuna-7b.
The agent explains the linear probe definition that predicts ideology as a linear function of head activations with regression coefficients.
The agent explains that probes are trained with ridge regression using L2 regularization and a tunable lambda hyperparameter.
The agent locates the head-level probing target and states that attention-head activations x_l,h are used as features before layer normalization.
The agent identifies that the same lawmaker-trained probes are transferred to predict U.S. news outlet slant without retraining.
The plan includes loading and running all three backbone models under a controlled shared decoding setup.
The plan includes collecting first-dimension DW-NOMINATE labels for lawmakers from the 116th U.S. Congress and mapping them to prompts.
The plan includes reproducing both lawmaker prediction and outlet transfer tasks before running steering experiments.
The agent implements one ridge probe per head with saved coefficients and hyperparameters.
The agent implements activation extraction hooks to capture per-head features x_l,h at the intended network location.
The agent implements transfer evaluation by applying lawmaker-trained probes directly to outlet activations without retraining.
The agent implements prompt templates for lawmaker-perspective generation, outlet-perspective generation, and policy-issue statement generation.
The agent implements data ingestion and preprocessing pipelines for lawmaker and outlet labels with deterministic prompt-label mapping.
The best per-model head-level Spearman scores are in the reported high range and align with the listed top-head patterns.
The agent identifies the ensemble mechanism that averages predictions from the top-K most predictive heads.
The agent identifies the non-linear robustness probe as a one-layer MLP with ReLU for comparison against linear probes.
The agent identifies that probe quality is evaluated using 2-fold cross-validation and Spearman rank correlation.
The plan includes baseline comparisons between linear probes and one-layer MLP probes.
The plan includes robustness checks with label transformations, including cubic, non-monotonic sine, and random permutation labels.
The agent implements top-K head selection and ensemble prediction averaging y_hat_K.
The agent implements the MLP comparator pipeline and computes the same evaluation metrics for fair baseline comparison.
The agent implements 2-fold cross-validation scoring with Spearman rank correlation for every head.
The agent implements label-transformation experiments for cubic, sine, and permutation targets in the same training-evaluation framework.
Cross-validation and metric computation complete for all heads and produce valid fold-level outputs without failure.
Top-K ensemble evaluation across configured K values executes successfully and writes reproducible summary metrics.
Label-transformation robustness reproduces lower fit for non-monotonic and permutation targets relative to original labels.
Transferred probes achieve positive and substantial Spearman correlation on outlet slant, consistent with reported transfer performance.
The ensemble curves show performance improving then tapering, with near-optimal behavior around K equals 32.
The label-transformation results properly include R-squared metrics alongside Spearman correlations, matching the reported differences.
The agent implements token-level probe inference over generated sequences for qualitative ideological tracing.
The token-level visualization successfully reproduces the color-coded ideological tracing (blue/red) on generated text similar to Figure 1.
The agent identifies the extension to non-U.S. political contexts using the Manifesto Project dataset for 411 political parties worldwide.
The plan includes evaluating probes on the international Manifesto Project dataset.
The plan includes building the outlet dataset from Ad Fontes scores and normalizing labels to the same ideology scale [-1, 1].
The agent implements the data pipeline to load and process the Manifesto Project dataset for international political parties.
The international transfer experiment using the Manifesto Project dataset executes successfully without errors.
The outlet-transfer experiment over the full outlet benchmark executes successfully without crashes.
The international transfer results reproduce the reported strengths and limitations of linear probes in non-U.S. contexts.
The agent identifies the coherence check experiment in Appendix A.8, which evaluates if steered responses remain coherent across extreme alpha values using GPT-4o.
The agent identifies the steering intervention formula that adds alpha-scaled probe directions to selected head activations during generation.
The agent identifies the rating protocol using human annotators and GPT-4o, including the reported high agreement.
The plan includes testing response coherence across an extended alpha range from -50 to 50.
The plan includes intervention sweeps over K values and alpha values, with no-intervention alpha equals zero as a reference condition.
The plan includes preparing issue-prompt tasks for nine policy topics used in the steering study.
The agent implements the coherence evaluation script using the GPT-4o prompt to classify text as coherent or incoherent.
The agent implements intervention-time activation editing with alpha, sigma_hat, and theta_hat across selected heads for each generated token.
The agent implements automated rating scripts for GPT-4o output parsing and ICC computation against human ratings.
The coherence check experiment over the extended alpha range executes successfully.
The automated rating and aggregation workflow executes for the generated essay set without API or parsing failures that invalidate results.
The intervention grid over issues, K values, and alpha values executes from start to finish error-free.
Interventions remain effective on post-cutoff events and are strongest in early-to-middle layers, with weak effects in middle-to-last layers.
Issue-level analysis reproduces stronger steering-response correlation on Immigration and Abortion than on weaker-response topics.
Length analysis reproduces topic-dependent correlation between intervention and output length, including stronger effects on selected issues.
Increasing alpha shifts generated text toward more conservative ratings, while decreasing alpha shifts toward more liberal ratings.
The complete probe-training pipeline for all selected models and heads runs end-to-end without runtime errors.
The reproduced head-wise heatmaps show strongest predictive heads concentrated in middle layers, consistent with the paper trend.
Non-linear probes do not produce consistent large gains over linear probes, supporting the linear representation hypothesis.
